title: Marbella Cheap Boat Rental: 2026 Prices and Tips
html:
Marbella offers surprisingly affordable options for renting a boat on the Costa del Sol, with shared tours from EUR 85 per person and small motorboats from EUR 250 for a half-day, all bookable through BoatHire24, a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Marbella or list your own.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Marbella?

Prices start at EUR 85 per person for group tours and EUR 250 for a half-day on a small motorboat. For a full breakdown of affordable options, you can compare verified listings and check rates for yacht charter in Marbella on BoatHire24, which covers everything from budget-friendly to luxury vessels.

Where to set off in Marbella

Most boat rentals depart from Puerto Deportivo de Marbella, also known as the La Bajadilla marina, a central point with easy access to the sea. Puerto Banús is the iconic luxury harbour, but it also offers competitive charter rates, especially if you avoid peak months. For a quieter start, consider Marbella’s Real Club Náutico or the nearby marina at Cabopino, which is closer to the dunes of Artola. Day cruises often head to the sandy beaches of El Pirata or further south to the Costa del Sol’s hidden coves. If you have time, a run from Marbella to the rock of Gibraltar covers about 40 nautical miles.

Which boat to rent

Your choice depends on group size, budget, and whether you want to skipper yourself. Here is a breakdown of popular types:

  1. Small motorboat (6-8 guests), best for a budget half-day exploring the coast from EUR 250.
  2. Catamaran (10-12 guests), stable and spacious for full-day parties from EUR 800.
  3. Carving rib (6-10 guests), fast and fun for water sports or coastal hops from EUR 400.
  4. Crewed motor yacht (8-12 guests), luxury with a captain from EUR 1,200, such as the Mefasa 90; see the Mefasa 90 charter page for full details.
  5. Sailboat (6-8 guests), for a relaxed, eco-friendly cruise from EUR 450.

Cost breakdown for a cheap boat rental in Marbella

Prices vary by season, boat type, and duration. Use this quick guide to plan:

  • Shared guided tour: from EUR 85 per person, typically 3-4 hours.
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours): EUR 250-450 for up to 6 guests.
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hours): EUR 800-1,500 for up to 12 guests.
  • Crewed yacht full-day: from EUR 1,200 for a 50-foot model.

When to visit and do you need a licence?

High season runs from June to September, with July and August being the busiest; the local Poniente wind often picks up in the afternoon. For UK and EU licence holders, an ICC or equivalent is required for bareboat charters, but the operator confirms exact requirements; many small motorboats under 15 hp do not need a licence.

To avoid crowds and get better rates, consider renting in May or early October, when the water is still warm and the breeze is lighter along the Costa del Sol.

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 85, 1,200+ per day
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Trip length: 3 hours to full day
  • Guest capacity: 6 to 12 people typical
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Marbella

Can I rent a boat in Marbella without a licence?

Yes, many operators offer smaller motorboats under 15 hp that do not require a licence, or you can hire a skipper for larger vessels.

What is the cheapest month to rent a boat in Marbella?

October and May offer the lowest prices, with high season rates applying from June to September.

Are there any hidden fees when renting a boat in Marbella?

Fuel, mooring fees, and optional extras like a skipper or water toys are usually charged separately, so confirm the total upfront.

How far can I cruise from Marbella in a half-day trip?

A half-day cruise typically covers 10 to 20 nautical miles, reaching places like Cabopino or the coast of Benalmádena.

Whether you are a first-time renter or a seasoned boater, you can compare options and book with confidence on the BoatHire24 marketplace. If you own a boat in Marbella, you can also list it for free and start earning from the growing demand in 2026.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Marbella and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
